name: common-stocks-and-uncommon-profits
description: Philip A. Fisher's Common Stocks and Uncommon Profits — systematic toolkit for identifying exceptional growth companies through the Scuttlebutt research method and 15-Point framework
useCase: Research and invest in outstanding growth companies using Fisher's Scuttlebutt method, 15-Point checklist, and long-term holding discipline
knownRisks: Scuttlebutt requires access to industry sources — may not be feasible for all investors or all companies
The 15 points are qualitative, not quantitative — applying them consistently requires judgment that improves with experience
Long holding periods require emotional discipline that most investors lack
Fisher's anti-diversification stance increases volatility risk
